headline: Coffee Stocks Lose Steam as McDonald's Readies New Drinks
summary: A push by McDonald's into energy drinks and specialty sodas is jolting other caffeine-linked stocks. Shares of coffee chains Dutch Bros and Black Rock Coffee fell 4.1% and 5.1%, respectively, on Monday after The Wall Street Journal reported that McDonald’s is launching new refresher and dirty-soda beverages across its U.

headline: McDonald’s Stock Is Down Over 6% This Month but Wall Street Sees a Path Back to $344
summary: McDonald’s (NYSE: MCD) currently trades near $304, while the average analyst price target is up at $344.79, a gap of roughly 13% from current levels. McDonald’s operates the world’s largest fast-food network, with roughly 90% of its restaurants franchised, generating consistent cash flows that have made it a core holding for income investors for decades. ... McDonald’s Stock Is Down Over 6% This Month but Wall Street Sees a Path Back to $344

headline: Conagra Brands finds new CEO, McDonald's to add energy drinks to menus
summary: Market Catalysts Host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma track several of the day's top trending stock tickers, including Conagra Brands (CAG) hiring former J.M. Smucker (SJM) COO John Brase to be the company's new CEO, McDonald's (MCD) adding new energy drink items to the menu, and Somnigroup International (SGI) acquiring Leggett & Platt (LEG) in a $2.5 billion deal.

headline: Starbucks upgraded at Jefferies on improved turnaround visibility
summary: Investing.com -- Jefferies upgraded Starbucks Corp. to Hold from Underperform on Monday, citing reduced international exposure following the completion of a China joint venture deal and early signs of stabilization in the company’s core U.S. business.
